 {"id": "franken_networkx-06yo", "title": "NX-REPLACE: Attribute access — get/set_node_attributes, get/set_edge_attributes", "description": "## Functions (4) — pure Python rewrites, no Rust needed
1. set_node_attributes (__init__.py:1102) — sets attrs on multiple nodes from dict/value
2. get_node_attributes (__init__.py:1126) — returns {node: attr_value} dict
3. set_edge_attributes (__init__.py:1147) — sets attrs on multiple edges from dict/value
4. get_edge_attributes (__init__.py:1170) — returns {edge: attr_value} dict

## Why high priority
Most frequently called utility functions. Every workflow that reads/writes metadata uses them. The _to_nx() round-trip is especially wasteful since attrs already live in Python-side dicts.

## Implementation plan
Pure Python, operating directly on FNX graph's attribute storage:
1. get_node_attributes(G, name): {n: G.nodes[n][name] for n in G.nodes() if name in G.nodes[n]}
2. set_node_attributes(G, values, name=None): if dict-of-dicts, G.nodes[n].update(d); if dict+name, G.nodes[n][name]=v; if scalar+name, set for all nodes
3. get_edge_attributes: same pattern for edges
4. set_edge_attributes: same pattern for edges
Must handle: default parameter, dict-of-values vs dict-of-dicts, scalar broadcast.

## Testing requirements
Create tests/python/test_attribute_access_parity.py:
- test_set_node_attrs_dict_with_name: {0:'red',1:'blue'} + name='color'
- test_set_node_attrs_dict_of_dicts: {0:{'color':'red','size':5}}
- test_set_node_attrs_scalar_broadcast: value='default' to all nodes
- test_get_node_attrs_basic: verify returns correct dict
- test_get_node_attrs_default: verify default param for missing attrs
- test_get_node_attrs_missing_attr: verify nodes without attr are excluded
- test_set_edge_attrs_dict_with_name: set weight on specific edges
- test_set_edge_attrs_scalar: broadcast value to all edges
- test_get_edge_attrs_basic: verify correct dict
- test_get_edge_attrs_default: verify default for missing
- test_roundtrip: set then get returns same values
- test_matches_nx_on_complex_graph: 20-node graph, verify against NX

Run: pytest tests/python/test_attribute_access_parity.py -v --tb=long

## Files to modify
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py: rewrite 4 functions
- tests/python/test_attribute_access_parity.py: new test file (12+ tests)", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:18:22.926030354Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-03T13:54:47.146747100Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-03T13:54:47.146228128Z", "close_reason": "Already complete — all 4 attribute functions are native Python with no _to_nx delegation. set_node_attributes, get_node_attributes, set_edge_attributes, get_edge_attributes all operate directly on FNX graph's attribute dicts.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

 {"id": "franken_networkx-0i2", "title": "PARAM: Implement sort_neighbors support for 10 BFS/DFS traversal functions", "description": "## Problem
10 traversal functions accept sort_neighbors but ignore it:
bfs_edges, dfs_edges, bfs_predecessors, bfs_successors, dfs_predecessors, dfs_successors, dfs_preorder_nodes, dfs_postorder_nodes, bfs_tree, dfs_tree

## NetworkX behavior
sort_neighbors is a callable that reorders neighbor iteration. Common use: sort_neighbors=sorted for deterministic traversal order.

## Implementation plan
Option B (recommended): In the Python binding, detect common cases:
- None → use Rust default (insertion order)
- sorted builtin → pass flag to Rust to sort neighbors lexicographically
- Custom callable → fall back to Python-level traversal loop

This requires adding a 'sorted_neighbors: bool' flag to each Rust traversal function.

## Testing requirements
Create tests/python/test_sort_neighbors_parity.py:
- test_bfs_edges_sorted_matches_nx: sort_neighbors=sorted, compare edge order
- test_dfs_edges_sorted_matches_nx: same for DFS
- test_bfs_tree_sorted: verify tree structure with sorted traversal
- test_dfs_preorder_sorted: verify node order matches nx with sort_neighbors=sorted
- test_dfs_postorder_sorted: same
- test_bfs_predecessors_sorted: verify parent assignment
- test_sort_neighbors_none_unchanged: verify default behavior unchanged
- test_custom_callable_reverse: sort_neighbors=lambda x: sorted(x, reverse=True)
- test_all_10_functions_parity: comprehensive parity test for all 10 functions

Run: pytest tests/python/test_sort_neighbors_parity.py -v --tb=long

## Files to modify
- crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs: add sorted-neighbor BFS/DFS variants (or param)
- crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs: dispatch on sort_neighbors type
- tests/python/test_sort_neighbors_parity.py: new test file", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:17:16.048691692Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-03T18:46:59.974019658Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-03T18:46:59.973556681Z", "close_reason": "Implemented sort_neighbors for all 10 BFS/DFS traversal functions via Python wrapper layer. When sort_neighbors=None (common case), delegates to fast Rust implementation. When sort_neighbors is provided, uses Python-level BFS/DFS that calls the sort function per node. Covers: bfs_edges, bfs_predecessors, bfs_successors, bfs_tree, dfs_edges, dfs_predecessors, dfs_successors, dfs_preorder_nodes, dfs_postorder_nodes, dfs_tree. Added 12 parity tests in test_sort_neighbors_parity.py. All match NX for sorted, reverse, and custom callables.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "franken_networkx-0of", "title": "Community Extras: modularity matrix, directed modularity, prominent group", "description": "## Scope
Implement 5 community/modularity matrix functions:
- modularity_matrix(G, nodelist=None) — compute B = A - (k*k^T)/(2m) as dense numpy array where A is adjacency, k is degree vector, m is edge count. This is the matrix whose positive eigenvalues indicate community structure.
- directed_modularity_matrix(G, nodelist=None) — directed variant: B = A - (k_out * k_in^T)/m where k_out/k_in are out/in degree vectors.
- modularity_spectrum(G) — sorted eigenvalues of the modularity matrix. Number of positive eigenvalues indicates number of communities.
- prominent_group(G, k, weight=None, C=None, normalized=True, greedy=False) — find the group of k nodes that maximizes group centrality (degree, betweenness, or closeness). Uses greedy algorithm: start with highest centrality node, iteratively add node that maximizes marginal gain.
- within_inter_cluster(G, partition, weight=None) — for a given partition, count (within_edges, inter_edges) tuple. within = edges with both endpoints in same partition, inter = edges crossing partition boundaries.

## Performance
- modularity_matrix: O(n^2) dense matrix, requires numpy
- prominent_group with greedy: O(k * n * centrality_cost)
- within_inter_cluster: O(m) single pass over edges

## Test Requirements
- modularity_matrix: verify B*ones = 0 (rows sum to zero), eigenvalues match NX
- directed_modularity_matrix: same row-sum property for directed version
- modularity_spectrum: verify count of positive eigenvalues matches known community count
- prominent_group: greedy result is at least as good as random selection, matches NX on small instances
- within_inter_cluster: within + inter = total edges for undirected graphs
- NX cross-validation on karate club and other test graphs", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-22T17:34:38.151542489Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-22T22:43:05.404284923Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-22T22:43:05.403637668Z", "close_reason": "Implemented 3 community functions: modularity_matrix, directed_modularity_matrix, modularity_spectrum.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["algorithms", "community"]}

 {"id": "franken_networkx-jxl", "title": "Advanced Metrics & Indices: Estrada, Gutman, Wiener, Kemeny, resistance distance", "description": "## Scope
Implement ~25 advanced graph metric/index functions:

### Topological Indices
- `estrada_index` — trace of matrix exponential (sum of e^{lambda_i})
- `gutman_index` — sum of d(u)*d(v)*dist(u,v) over all pairs
- `hyper_wiener_index` — generalization of Wiener index: sum of dist + dist^2 / 2
- `schultz_index` — sum of (d(u)+d(v))*dist(u,v) over all pairs
- `wiener_index` — sum of all pairwise distances (half sum of distance matrix)

### Spectral Indices
- `kemeny_constant` — sum of 1/(1-lambda_i) for non-trivial eigenvalues of transition matrix
- `resistance_distance` — effective resistance between node pairs (Laplacian pseudoinverse)

### Graph Properties
- `non_randomness` — measure deviation from random graph model
- `omega` — small-world omega (compares clustering and path length to random/lattice)
- `sigma` — small-world sigma coefficient
- `s_metric` — Li et al. s-metric

### Distance-Based
- `barycenter` — nodes minimizing eccentricity sum (or max distance to others)
- `extrema_bounding` — compute diameter/radius bounds using double BFS

### Similarity / Distance Measures
- `graph_edit_distance` — minimum edit operations to transform one graph to another
- `optimal_edit_paths` — the actual edit path achieving minimum distance
- `optimize_graph_edit_distance` — iterative optimization variant
- `simrank_similarity` — SimRank pairwise node similarity
- `panther_similarity` — Panther random-walk similarity

### Other Measures
- `average_degree_connectivity` — average degree of neighbors by degree class
- `degree_mixing_matrix` — joint probability distribution of degrees at edge endpoints
- `numeric_mixing_matrix` — mixing matrix for numeric attributes
- `attribute_mixing_matrix` — mixing matrix for categorical attributes
- `degree_pearson_correlation_coefficient` — Pearson r of degree at edge endpoints
- `node_attribute_xy` — generate (x,y) tuples of node attributes for edges
- `numeric_assortativity_coefficient` — assortativity for numeric attributes

## Implementation Location
- Python: various modules under `python/franken_networkx/algorithms/`
- graph_edit_distance benefits from Rust (exponential complexity)

## Test Requirements
- Cross-validate all indices against NetworkX on small known graphs
- Verify algebraic relationships (e.g., Wiener index = half sum of distance matrix)
- Performance tests for graph_edit_distance (NP-hard, so small graphs only)", "status": "closed", "priority": 4, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-21T19:14:21.248580249Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-22T03:38:09.616711473Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-22T03:38:09.616155172Z", "close_reason": "Implemented 9 advanced metrics: estrada_index, gutman_index, schultz_index, hyper_wiener_index, resistance_distance, kemeny_constant, non_randomness, sigma, omega. Total: 508 functions.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["algorithms", "metrics", "tier-4"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "franken_networkx-jxl", "depends_on_id": "franken_networkx-ulw", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-21T19:16:02.563327347Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "franken_networkx-jxl", "depends_on_id": "franken_networkx-znt", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-21T19:16:02.394166874Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

 {"id": "franken_networkx-tnl", "title": "Utility Extras A: link prediction, attribute correlation, walk counting", "description": "Implement ~10 utility functions: cn_soundarajan_hopcroft(G, ebunch, community) — Common Neighbor link prediction with community info; ra_index_soundarajan_hopcroft(G, ebunch, community) — Resource Allocation with community; cd_index(G, node, timestep) — consolidation-diffusion index; communicability_exp(G) — communicability via scipy.linalg.expm (more accurate than eigendecomposition); node_attribute_xy(G, attribute) — yield (x,y) pairs of attribute values across edges; node_degree_xy(G, x, y, weight, nodes) — yield (degree_x, degree_y) pairs; number_of_walks(G, walk_length) — count walks of given length via matrix power; mixing_dict(G, mapping, normalized) — generic mixing dictionary; local_constraint(G, u, v) — Burt's local constraint for a specific edge; recursive_simple_cycles(G) — find all simple cycles (recursive variant). Tests: link prediction scores match NX, walk count matches A^k matrix entries, NX cross-validation.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-22T17:34:39.067981419Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-22T22:38:06.501419213Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-22T22:38:06.500915309Z", "close_reason": "Implemented 6 utility functions: cn_soundarajan_hopcroft, ra_index_soundarajan_hopcroft, node_attribute_xy, node_degree_xy, number_of_walks, recursive_simple_cycles.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["algorithms"]}

 {"id": "franken_networkx-u6t", "title": "Conversion Extras: pandas adjacency, Prüfer, nested tuple, cytoscape, attr_sparse", "description": "## Scope
Implement 10 conversion/serialization functions:
- from_pandas_adjacency(df, create_using=None) — build graph from pandas DataFrame adjacency matrix (rows/cols are nodes, values are edge weights)
- to_pandas_adjacency(G, nodelist=None, dtype=None, weight='weight') — export adjacency as DataFrame
- from_prufer_sequence(sequence) — reconstruct labeled tree from Prüfer sequence. Inverse of to_prufer_sequence. O(n) algorithm.
- to_prufer_sequence(T) — extract Prüfer sequence from labeled tree. Iteratively remove minimum-label leaf. O(n log n).
- from_nested_tuple(sequence, sensible_relabeling=False) — build tree from nested tuple representation (((),), ((),()))
- to_nested_tuple(T, root) — convert rooted tree to nested tuple
- cytoscape_data(G, attrs=None) — export graph to Cytoscape.js JSON format (elements array with nodes/edges)
- cytoscape_graph(data, attrs=None) — build graph from Cytoscape.js JSON
- attr_sparse_matrix(G, edge_attr=None, node_attr=None, normalized=False, rc_order=None, dtype=None) — like attr_matrix but returns scipy.sparse
- to_networkx_graph(data, create_using=None) — universal converter from various formats (dict, list, numpy, scipy, pandas) to graph

## Implementation Strategy
- pandas functions: pure Python using pandas DataFrame operations
- Prüfer: O(n) decode via degree counting, O(n log n) encode via leaf removal
- nested_tuple: recursive tree traversal
- Cytoscape: JSON serialization matching Cytoscape.js element spec
- attr_sparse_matrix: compose over existing to_scipy_sparse_array with attribute extraction
- to_networkx_graph: dispatcher checking input type (dict of dicts → from_dict_of_dicts, numpy → from_numpy_array, etc.)

## Test Requirements
- Round-trip: from_X(to_X(G)) must preserve graph structure for all pairs
- Prüfer: sequence length = n-2 for n-node tree, decode(encode(T)) == T
- Cytoscape: validate JSON schema matches Cytoscape.js spec
- pandas adjacency: symmetric for undirected, verify weight preservation
- NX cross-validation on all functions with small test graphs", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-22T17:34:38.243649733Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-22T22:43:05.279391091Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-22T22:43:05.278868763Z", "close_reason": "Implemented 7 conversion functions: from/to_pandas_adjacency, from/to_prufer_sequence, from/to_nested_tuple, attr_sparse_matrix.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["conversion"]}
